creator: portmaster@bsdforge.com
text:
On the following hardware, an upgrade from
FreeBSD 15.0-CURRENT #0 main-n267640-7a4d1d1df0b2:
Thu Jan 18 04:04:32 UTC 2024
to
FreeBSD 15.0-CURRENT #0 main-n268793-220ee18f1964:
Thu Mar 14 02:58:39 UTC 2024
resulted in the inability to use any other tty than
ttyv0. ps waux | grep getty, indicates getty is
servicing all of them, as does ls /dev indicate that they
are all there. /etc/ttys and /etc/gettytb are identical
to previous versions of CURRENT. I have nothing in rc.conf(5)
that would have any affect. The use of any other tty than ttyv0
is also unavailable while booted to any of the FreeBSD install media.
My most recent attempt is from:
FreeBSD 15.0-CURRENT #0 main-n269036-6baddb6b1176:
Fri Mar 29 10:19:43 UTC 2024
Which fails in the same manner.

The hardware affected is as follows:

CPU: 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i3-1215U (2496.00-MHz K8-class CPU)

IdeaPad 3 17IAU7

Environmental information:

Thank you.
